summary: Freeze the M1 native-runtime candidate: stage normalized multi-file text/binary projects; render real 3D STL or 2D SVG geometry; provide full-quality native exports; stream and retain ordered engine output; cancel and reap process trees; debounce and supersede renders; expose truthful engine recovery, preview/full controls, console history, editor settings, focus-aware Appendix D bindings, and working Edit-menu actions; and record the remaining owner decisions and formal gate blockers without advancing M1.
specSections: Section 2.7 questions, decisions, and milestone boundary
Section 4.1 C1 editor settings and keybindings
Section 7 V-2 dependency and license policy
Appendix A EngineService request and result contracts

externalInputs.purpose: Verify that OpenSCAD 2021.01 remains the latest official stable release rather than silently switching the M1 engine pin to a development snapshot.
Confirm the official site still separates the 2021.01 stable artifacts from nightly/development builds.
Check official command-line export and camera behavior used to isolate the lossless CameraPose question.
Cross-check the documented OpenSCAD CLI camera forms before rejecting rather than truncating explicit camera requests.
Resolve and pin the approved official CodeMirror command extension used by C1 editor actions.
Resolve and pin the approved official CodeMirror search extension used by find, replace, and go-to-line actions.
Use the approved Rust ecosystem crate already present transitively as a direct dependency for RAII project workspaces and anonymous complete-log spooling.

decisions.decision: Validate every logical project path before engine start, reject platform aliases and file/directory collisions, create one isolated project root, and write text or binary bytes without coercion before invoking OpenSCAD with that root as CWD.
decisions.rationale: The engine must resolve include/use and imported assets exactly from the request map without path traversal, silent overwrites, encoding damage, or dependence on the user's real project directory.

decisions.topic: Native geometry and export result selection
decisions.decision: Use engine-produced binary STL for 3D results, fall back to engine-produced SVG only for the pinned engine's explicit 2D top-level response, calculate typed bounds from the selected artifact, and permit exports only through full-quality commands.
decisions.rationale: Both quality levels must return real rendered geometry, 2D/3D selection must follow engine output, and exports must never reuse preview overrides.

decisions.decision: Export default-camera PNG through the pinned CLI but reject an explicit CameraPose with a typed invalid-project failure while Q-0021 is open; never discard CameraPose.up or invent an Euler/projection conversion.
decisions.rationale: The stable CLI cannot represent all normative camera fields losslessly, so an explicit failure is more truthful than a plausibly valid but incorrectly framed image.

decisions.topic: Native process lifecycle and output capture
decisions.decision: Read stdout/stderr concurrently in fixed chunks with incremental UTF-8 carry, assign one monotonic event sequence, spool each subprocess's complete interleaved output to an anonymous file, bound its live replay with explicit limit/cancel/timeout markers, poll cancellation/timeouts independently of consumer speed through final drain, preserve logs through artifact failures, and kill and reap the complete process tree before returning or unwinding.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the normative complete rawLog while preventing ordinary streaming/UI memory growth and ensuring a slow consumer cannot defeat supersession or timeout cleanup; Q-0022 isolates the remaining complete-string materialization tension.

decisions.decision: Keep cancellation tokens in a synchronized job registry keyed by request id, make cancellation idempotent, preserve early cancellation until registration, cancel a same-id predecessor, remove only the exact completed token, and clean up registry state after worker join failure.
decisions.rationale: The desktop boundary must not lose rapid supersession, cancel the wrong replacement job, or leave a completed/join-failed job reachable indefinitely.

decisions.decision: Debounce automatic preview by the typed default of 800 ms, cancel an in-flight job when a newer source snapshot supersedes it, retain every run in console history, and allow only the current source/job identity to replace the active geometry or current diagnostics.
decisions.rationale: Rapid editing must produce one current completion without hiding cancelled evidence or allowing an older native result to overwrite newer source state.

decisions.decision: Retain per-run ordered output and diagnostics under one global 10,000-line UI cap, reconstruct rawLog only when no stream event was received, preserve the live run shell on Clear, suppress cleared fallback resurrection, and keep subsequent events visible.
decisions.rationale: The console must be useful during and after a run without unbounded scrollback, duplicated fallback logs, stale resurrection after a user clear, or loss of later output.

decisions.decision: Preserve the mandated bundled/configured/environment/PATH discovery order and model checking, unavailable, invalid-config, and ready as explicit UI states; retry one configured path with pending and rejected-path feedback while labeling the editor buffer temporary until Q-0012 is resolved.
decisions.rationale: Dependency recovery must not show contradictory checking/unavailable states, silently swallow an invalid path, or promise save capability that the M1 owner boundary has not defined.

decisions.decision: Apply typed editor defaults through live CodeMirror compartments, keep the complete Appendix D registry centrally rebindable, canonicalize binding collisions with a platform-specific primary modifier, and prevent handled editor/viewer events from falling through to global commands.
decisions.rationale: M1 can provide the C1 configuration seam before the M2 settings UI while ensuring one keystroke has one focus-appropriate meaning on Windows/Linux and macOS.

decisions.decision: Route implemented Edit-menu and keyboard actions through one typed CodeMirror execution path, perform real multi-selection for the Appendix D pointer binding, and surface go-to-definition as a visible typed unavailable outcome until its owning project-symbol capability lands.
decisions.rationale: A routed command is not acceptance evidence if it is a silent no-op; supported actions must mutate editor state and deferred actions must say why they are unavailable.

decisions.decision: Retain OpenSCAD 2021.01 as the M1 engine pin because it remains the latest official stable release; treat 2026 development artifacts as nightlies and change the pin only through A-5 when parity, color export, or a confirmed engine defect requires it.
decisions.rationale: Using a newer date-stamped development artifact merely because it is newer would violate the default stable-release rule and weaken reproducibility before an A-5 trigger is demonstrated.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-4.5 project staging, AC-4.b parameters, and 2D/3D/export artifact behavior
testEvidence.test: Real-engine fixtures cover nested include resolution, byte-exact imported STL, cross-file errors, typed parameter geometry changes, 2D SVG bounds, 3D STL, default-camera PNG, export mappings, and hostile/colliding logical paths.

red.outcome: RED evidence recorded during implementation (original execution timestamps not retained): collision, parameter, camera, export, and multi-file assertions initially failed or reached the wrong error boundary before staging, typed definitions, artifact selection, and typed rejection were implemented.

green.outcome: GREEN: 24 unit, 2 export-integration, and 10 project-integration tests passed (36 total), including Win32 ADS/trailing/reserved-name safety and complete-log preservation through a malformed engine artifact.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-4.8/FR-4.9 bounded streaming, complete rawLog, timeout, and cancellation
testEvidence.test: High-volume helpers emit a 2 MiB newline-free record, 5,120 small records, split Unicode, malformed/missing artifacts, slow callbacks, cancellation, descendants, and a following successful process.

red.outcome: RED evidence recorded during implementation: 2,097,308 bytes were retained live, 5,120 records had no truncation signal, and a slow consumer delayed cancellation to 6.3877676 seconds; review also reproduced split-code-point and post-artifact rawLog gaps.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 36 native tests passed, including fixed-chunk/spool budgets, split UTF-8, final-drain cancellation/timeout, slow-consumer responsiveness, callback-unwind process-tree reaping, truthful markers, complete artifact-failure logs, descendant cleanup, and immediate following-process success.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-4.4/AC-4.e scheduling and C8 console retention
testEvidence.test: Runtime tests exercise 800 ms debounce, one completion after rapid edits, in-flight cancellation, stale-result suppression, per-run output ordering, waiting/clear/fallback behavior, and the 10,000-line cap.

red.outcome: RED: implementation assertions initially lacked waiting-state behavior; the final falsification test also proved that clearing a quiet in-flight run resurrected its complete rawLog on finish.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 5 focused console-state tests passed, including no resurrection after Clear, later-event visibility, genuine no-event rawLog fallback, ordering, and global retention.

testEvidence.criterion: Appendix D focus-aware keybindings and truthful deferred commands
testEvidence.test: Editor/global collision tests, explicit Control/Meta platform tests, actual editor outcomes, and Workbench status/history assertions cover F12, render/layout fallthrough, and pointer multi-selection.

red.outcome: RED recorded by the focused TDD pass: 3 assertions failed for missing F12 status, duplicate console dispatch, and Control incorrectly satisfying macOS Mod; a separate render test proved an allowed cancel/preview collision swallowed F5.

green.outcome: GREEN after final UI falsification: all 42 files / 391 tests passed; the focused command/app/keybinding set passed 7 files / 35 tests after typed outcomes, canonical collision checks, platform/fallthrough routing, cancelled-status truth, stable recovery drafts, and visible Edit actions.

testEvidence.criterion: Appendix D real multi-cursor pointer behavior
testEvidence.test: A real Chromium flow Alt+Clicks a second editor location and asserts two selections while a native CodeMirror Control-click still adds a third selection.

red.outcome: RED: the new Chromium assertion remained at one cursor because Alt+Click emitted only a handled command outcome and did not modify CodeMirror selection state.

green.outcome: GREEN: 4 Playwright flows passed, including Alt+Click producing two cursors and native Control-click producing three without regressing the responsive C0 cases.

testEvidence.criterion: FR-4.6 engine recovery and accessible C1 Edit-menu actions
testEvidence.test: App and menu tests cover checking/unavailable/invalid/ready states, pending/rejected retry, truthful temporary-buffer copy, runtime binding labels, pointer activation, arrow/Home/End traversal, and real CodeMirror action outcomes.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/app/App.test.tsx tests/ui/layout/WebMenuBar.test.tsx tests/ui/Workbench.editor-settings.test.tsx tests/application/diagnostics/parse-engine-log.test.ts --reporter=verbose
red.outcome: RED recorded by the focused TDD pass: engine checking simultaneously showed unavailable, invalid paths had no feedback, retries had no pending state, and Edit was disabled despite implemented commands.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 42 files / 391 tests passed, including explicit engine health/retry, temporary-buffer honesty, stable path drafts, working keyboard-navigable Edit actions, and diagnostic near-miss fuzz cases.

testEvidence.criterion: Desktop IPC project bytes, job cleanup, and typed failures
testEvidence.test: Rust shell tests decode text/binary project files, map typed failures, keep cancellation idempotent, preserve same-id replacements, honor cancellation before registration, and remove registry entries after join failure.

red.outcome: RED evidence recorded during implementation/review: the join-failure cleanup and same-id replacement cases initially failed, and final falsification found cancel-before-register was discarded.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 9 desktop-shell tests passed, including cancel-before-register consumption, bounded tombstone eviction, same-id token identity, join cleanup, and complete artifact rawLog mapping.

nearMisses.sought: Inspect ordinary repository workflow status while continuing to exclude the owner-supplied similarity system from local review.
nearMisses.confirmation: A later broad workflow wildcard accidentally printed the owner-supplied similarity workflow once. It was not executed, modified, used to derive product behavior, or inspected further; no comparison repository or prohibited editor source was accessed. All subsequent workflow checks used exact allowed paths or status-only exclusions.

questions: Q-0001 remains the M1 CI policy blocker: Rust Unicode-3.0 license expressions require the owner's allowlist or dependency-pin decision.
Q-0003 remains the M1 render-policy blocker: the preview-only $fn seam is not claimed as a true maximum until the owner defines cap semantics.
Q-0004/Q-0005/Q-0006 continue to park only final custom-theme import and contrast/color-policy ownership.
Q-0008 continues to park the final desktop per-project layout storage identity/location.
Q-0010/Q-0011/Q-0013/Q-0014/Q-0015/Q-0017 continue to define final command/settings/language/cross-file/web-binding acceptance boundaries while implemented seams remain usable and truthful.
Q-0012 is a formal M1 blocker: the temporary editor buffer cannot satisfy AC-1.d or AC-4.d until the owner decides whether M1 introduces a save port before C6.
Q-0016/Q-0018/Q-0019/Q-0020 continue to park stale-diagnostic, dirty/final-close, and unopened-file navigation behavior only.
Q-0021 parks explicit-camera PNG only; default-camera PNG and non-PNG native exports continue.
Q-0022 parks a hard bound for complete rawLog string materialization/spill growth while complete logs remain preserved and live replay is bounded.
GauntletGate all ran with valid first-run isolation and all five Full roles; its verdict is DO NOT ADVANCE until the blocker/critical owner decisions clear and the gate is rerun.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
